name: National Education Union
description: The largest education union in the UK and a TUC affiliate. Known for collective action, national campaigning and a strong in-school rep network. Covers teachers, leaders and support staff across England and Wales.
strength_1: Largest education union — strongest collective voice
strength_2: Active on pay, workload and education policy
strength_3: In-school reps in most secondary and many primary schools
strength_4: Covers teachers and support staff in the same union
consideration_1: Politically active — campaigns beyond narrow employment issues
consideration_2: Local branch subs added on top of the national rate
consideration_3: With size comes less personalised casework in some areas

name: NASUWT — The Teachers' Union
description: A teachers' union and TUC affiliate covering all four UK nations. Focuses on workplace rights, pay and conditions. Known for strong individual casework and willingness to take industrial action. Teachers and leaders only.
strength_1: One transparent fee — no local levies
strength_2: Strong track record on individual casework
strength_3: Active across England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland
consideration_1: Teachers and leaders only — not open to support staff
consideration_2: Smaller than NEU in many English schools

name: Edapt
description: An independent, apolitical alternative to a teaching union for people working in schools and colleges in England and Wales. Focuses on individual employment support rather than campaigning or industrial action.
strength_2: Apolitical — no campaigning or political affiliation
strength_4: Members can still join strikes as non-union participants
strength_5: Often lower monthly cost than traditional unions
consideration_1: Cannot organise or ballot for industrial action

name: Association of School and College Leaders
description: A leadership-focused union for senior leaders in schools and colleges. Offers specialist CPD, legal support and policy influence tailored to senior roles. Covers England, Wales and Northern Ireland.
strength_2: Strong policy influence at government level
strength_3: Tailored legal advice for leadership roles
strength_4: Professional Associate grade for aspiring leaders
consideration_1: Leaders only — not open to classroom teachers or support staff

name: National Association of Head Teachers
description: A union and TUC affiliate for school leaders at all levels, including middle and aspiring leaders. Active on school funding, accountability and headteacher-specific issues.
strength_1: Covers middle leaders upwards — broad leadership eligibility
consideration_3: Separate NAHT NI branch for Northern Ireland

name: Community Union
description: A general trade union and TUC affiliate covering workers across many sectors, including school support staff. Affiliated to the Labour Party. An affordable way to get full trade union protections.
strength_4: Covers support staff across the UK

name: GMB Union
description: One of the UK's largest general unions and a TUC affiliate. Covers school support staff — TAs, caretakers, admin, technicians. Strong on public sector pay and workplace rights.
strength_1: Large union with collective bargaining power
strength_4: Covers support staff across all four nations
consideration_3: Reps may not be education specialists

name: UNISON
description: The UK's largest public sector union and a TUC affiliate. Strong presence in schools for support staff — TAs, admin, caretakers — with a dedicated education sector team.
strength_1: Largest public sector union — significant bargaining power
strength_4: Fees scale with earnings — low-paid staff pay little
consideration_3: Earnings-based fees can be higher for better-paid roles

name: Unite the Union
description: One of the UK's largest general unions, with members across many sectors including some school support staff. Covers TAs, admin, facilities and similar roles. Politically active and a Labour Party affiliate.
strength_1: Large general union with significant bargaining power
strength_2: Active on pay and conditions across the public sector
strength_3: Member of the TUC; affiliated to the Labour Party
strength_4: Covers support staff across all four nations
consideration_1: Not education-specific — limited specialist schools knowledge
consideration_2: Few in-school reps compared to NEU and NASUWT — most school members are reached through general regional structures rather than dedicated school presence
consideration_4: Politically active and affiliated to the Labour Party

name: Educational Institute of Scotland
description: Scotland's largest teaching union and a founding STUC affiliate. Covers teachers and lecturers across Scottish schools, colleges and universities. Very active on pay and conditions in Scotland.
strength_2: Strong collective bargaining in Scotland's SNCT
strength_3: Active on pay, workload and policy
consideration_2: Teachers / lecturers / leaders — not support staff

name: Association of Headteachers and Deputes in Scotland
description: The professional association and recognised trade union for primary, special and nursery school leaders in Scotland. Specialist casework, CPD and representation for Scottish school leaders.
strength_1: Specialist support for Scottish primary and special-school leaders
consideration_2: Primary, special and nursery leaders only — not secondary (Scottish secondary leaders should look at School Leaders Scotland)

name: School Leaders Scotland
description: The professional association and recognised trade union for secondary school leaders in Scotland — present in over 90% of Scottish secondary schools and representing around 75% of headteachers. Fully affiliated to ASCL, which gives members full trade union rights and access to ASCL's wider legal infrastructure. Sits alongside AHDS (which serves primary, special and nursery leaders).
strength_1: Specialist support for Scottish secondary school leaders
strength_2: Dedicated field officer support and leadership-specific casework
strength_3: Policy access in Scottish secondary education — parliamentary bulletins, position statements
strength_4: ASCL affiliation provides expert legal advice and broader trade union backing
consideration_2: Secondary leaders only — Scottish primary leaders should look at AHDS
consideration_3: Less industrial-action presence than larger teacher unions

name: Scottish Secondary Teachers' Association
description: Scotland's only specialist union for secondary teachers, founded in 1944. Twin aims are to advance Scottish education and safeguard secondary teachers' interests, especially on pay and conditions. Active in SNCT pay negotiations alongside EIS and NASUWT — and active in industrial action when negotiation fails (recent ballots over pay 2022 and over class contact time 2025–26).
strength_2: Casework escalates to professional staff — similar model to NEU/NASUWT
strength_3: Free legal advice on professional matters; extended legal service via Thompsons
strength_4: 16 months free for new entrants — generous trainee/NQT offer
strength_5: Willing to ballot for industrial action when negotiation fails
consideration_2: Secondary teachers (and trainees) only — not for leaders or primary teachers
consideration_3: Smaller than EIS — less broad sector representation
consideration_4: Has a political fund framework, though no public evidence of party donations
cost_5_value: Free for 16 months from August of qualifying year

name: Irish National Teachers' Organisation
description: The largest teachers' union on the island of Ireland. In Northern Ireland it represents teachers and principals across both primary and post-primary (secondary) schools, with strong casework and professional development.
strength_1: Cross-border presence — strong in NI primary, post-primary and special schools
strength_2: Salary-based subscription deducted directly from payroll
strength_4: Free for trainees and newly qualified teachers in the first year
consideration_1: Northern Ireland only (separate INTO branches operate in Republic of Ireland)
consideration_2: Teachers and leaders only — not for non-teaching support staff
consideration_3: Salary-based fee means cost rises with pay scale
cost_4_value: Pro-rata on days worked (0.6% of pay)

name: Ulster Teachers' Union
description: A Northern Ireland teaching union with a focus on individual member support and a notably apolitical stance. Covers teachers and school leaders across NI.
consideration_2: Smaller than INTO or NASUWT NI

name: Undeb Cenedlaethol Athrawon Cymru
description: The Welsh-medium and Wales-focused teaching union. Bilingual service, strong on Welsh education policy, and casework delivered through the medium of Welsh or English.
strength_2: Specialist voice in Welsh education policy
consideration_2: Smaller than NEU Cymru or NASUWT Cymru
